notes: Spec asserts: state stays working through the queued-message ARRIVAL window, no →ready flicker when the queued input lands, no →waiting flicker, and the cycle still ends at ready once everything is consumed. opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — the daemon reads session state from the SQLite store keyed on ses_…, so a contiguous working span + clean working→ready close are the same store-read signals basic-turn proves. The recipe drives opencode's live TUI under tmux (run_live, routed by the mid_turn_send step): a slow first prompt (~30-45s on the local LM Studio model) keeps the ses_… turn in flight, then mid_turn_send types a SECOND message + Enter into the composer WITHOUT bumping the turn baseline — opencode silently QUEUES it (its documented default) and does NOT write it to the SQLite part/message tables until consumed, so during the in-flight + injection window the daemon's last store event stays turn-internal and the session stays working with no flicker. The queued message is then consumed as a NEW turn (a step-finish reason=stop closes turn1, a second working→ready arc runs turn2). session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn_start (matching working) establishes the session identity the later phases chain off. The queued_window phase pins the no-flicker-on-arrival invariant with duration_at_least_ms 3000 anchored to turn_start (the in-flight window before turn1 completes), so the legal inter-turn ready between the two turns does not violate it. turn_end matches the FINAL ready after the queued turn drains. Whether opencode folds the queued message into the current turn or consumes it as a follow-up turn is NOT asserted — both shapes finish in ready (this recording observes consume-as-new-turn).

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch
PID bound to the ses_… session
First (slow) user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
A second user message is submitted mid-turn and queued. State must stay working on the same ses_… — no flicker to ready when the queued input lands, no flicker to waiting (spec bullets 1 and 2)
The cycle eventually ends at ready (spec bullet 3) — either the queued message folds into the current turn or is consumed as a follow-up turn; both shapes finish in ready on the same ses_…
